topic: Graph metadata relationship validation and entity quality analysis

focus: Corpus inventory, schema, parser, and backfill baseline
noveltyJustification: All four findings established first-pass corpus scope and runtime derivation behavior from code plus live scan.
ruledOut: Assuming the corpus size still matched the older 515-plus estimate

focus: Legacy-format coverage and distribution baseline
noveltyJustification: Three findings were new and one refined the initial inventory by separating JSON-invalid from legacy-loadable files.
ruledOut: Treating non-JSON files as automatically unparseable

focus: Validate depends_on edge resolution
noveltyJustification: Two dependency findings were new and one refined the manual relationship shape from strings vs objects.
ruledOut: Raw-schema-only assumption that depends_on entries were always object-shaped

focus: Validate children_ids integrity and cycle absence
noveltyJustification: Two findings were new and one corrected a false-positive scan path assumption from the initial shell prototype.
ruledOut: Joining dirname with children_ids values that already contain specs-root-relative paths

focus: Validate key_files existence across the corpus
noveltyJustification: Two findings were new and two added detail about path-resolution bases and dominant missing-reference types.
ruledOut: Repo-root-only existence checks as the only meaningful resolution baseline

focus: Entity duplication, noise patterns, and cap pressure
noveltyJustification: Three findings were new and one refined the interaction between key_files and entity insertion order.
ruledOut: Assuming newline cleanup in extracted entities was sufficient to prevent malformed names

focus: Status derivation rules versus implementation reality
noveltyJustification: Two status findings were new and two sharpened the mismatch root cause by tracing deriveStatus() and packet examples.
ruledOut: Reading markdown status tables as parser inputs

focus: Status normalization edge cases and backfill review flags
noveltyJustification: One finding was new and two refined status quality risks by connecting backfill review heuristics to live casing drift.
ruledOut: Assuming backfill review flags fully protect against semantic status drift

focus: Distribution analysis and timestamp freshness
noveltyJustification: Two findings were new and two consolidated prior scan outputs into concrete limit-pressure and freshness metrics.
ruledOut: Treating legacy epoch-like timestamps as current save evidence

focus: Cross-question synthesis and remediation priorities
noveltyJustification: This iteration added limited new raw evidence but produced a higher-value synthesis that prioritized the dominant systemic defects across all eight questions.
ruledOut: Further relationship scanning as the primary next step; quality remediation now dominates

reason: User requested iterations 11-20 focused on remediation design for status derivation, key-file sanitization, entity deduplication, legacy normalization, and trigger-phrase enforcement.

focus: `deriveStatus()` minimal patch and live status drift
noveltyJustification: Established the live 360-file baseline and isolated the exact one-function fallback needed for phase 001.
ruledOut: Parsing markdown status tables as a runtime signal

focus: Checklist completion as a secondary status signal
noveltyJustification: Connected the parser gap to the existing Spec Kit completion contract and measured the safer precedence split.
ruledOut: Ad-hoc checklist regexes that ignore existing completion semantics

focus: Narrow key-file junk-pattern filter effectiveness
noveltyJustification: Measured the low-risk regex-only filter before adding the broader path-shape rule.
ruledOut: Treating the narrow command/version filter as sufficient by itself

focus: Bare non-canonical filename filtering for `key_files`
noveltyJustification: Identified the structural rule that removes most current misses after proper spec-relative resolution.
ruledOut: Relying on repo-root existence checks without spec-relative resolution

focus: Live duplicate-entity slot measurement
noveltyJustification: Converted the earlier duplicate-entity headline into exact current slot pressure against the live 360-file corpus.
ruledOut: Assuming duplicate pressure was limited to extracted entities only

focus: Exact basename-dedupe insertion point and canonical preference
noveltyJustification: Translated the collision measurements into the concrete callsites that phase 003 should patch first.
ruledOut: Adding the basename check only to extracted entities

focus: Legacy normalization safety and active-corpus drift
noveltyJustification: Rechecked the live corpus instead of repeating the stale 35-file assumption and mapped the safest no-op-friendly backfill flag.
ruledOut: Unconditional whole-tree backfill as the safest legacy cleanup path

focus: Trigger-phrase cap root cause and minimal enforcement
noveltyJustification: Pinned the overflow to a single missing slice call and quantified the current excess above the intended ceiling.
ruledOut: Looking for a hidden schema-level max-length guard

focus: Follow-on phase alignment and implementation order
noveltyJustification: Mapped the live code findings back onto the four child phases and identified phase 004 as drifted against the active branch.
ruledOut: Treating all four follow-on phases as equally urgent without rechecking live corpus drift

focus: Remediation synthesis and implementation guidance
noveltyJustification: Closed the reopened wave by converting the individual patch investigations into a risk-ranked implementation order and packet guidance.
ruledOut: Opening another discovery-only wave before landing parser fixes

note: Wave 2 investigated remediation shape rather than new defect discovery and updated the synthesis, strategy, dashboard, and follow-on packet guidance accordingly.

reason: User requested iterations 21-25 focused on convergence, exact filter logic, code-path tracing, and implementation-ready synthesis.

focus: Exact `key_files` sanitization predicate and corpus rerun
noveltyJustification: This pass converted the earlier filter recommendation into an exact predicate and refreshed the live corpus counts under that explicit rule.
ruledOut: Broad slash-path rejection as the default `key_files` sanitizer

focus: `deriveEntities()` basename de-duplication insertion path and canonical winner
noveltyJustification: Added one materially new conclusion beyond wave 2: the de-duplication helper must prefer canonical packet-doc paths on collision instead of blindly keeping the first basename seen.
ruledOut: A pure keep-first basename rule with no canonical replacement path

focus: Checklist-aware safer `deriveStatus()` pseudo-code
noveltyJustification: This iteration turned the earlier safer-status guidance into an exact decision tree anchored to the existing checklist-completion contract and the live 180/39/63 split.
ruledOut: Implementation-summary-only completion fallback when a checklist exists but is not complete

focus: Trigger-cap enforcement ownership across parser, schema, and backfill
noveltyJustification: Closed the ownership question by proving the omission is singular to parser derivation rather than shared across schema or backfill.
ruledOut: Backfill as the primary owner of the trigger-phrase cap

focus: Final sub-phase patch map and implementation handoff
noveltyJustification: No new defect class was discovered here; the value came from collapsing the prior waves into one implementation-ready change map for phases 001-004 plus the adjacent trigger-cap cleanup.
ruledOut: Another discovery-only wave before implementation

note: Wave 3 converted the earlier remediation findings into exact predicates, pseudo-code, and a per-phase line map for implementation handoff.

reason: User requested iterations 26-35 focused on post-implementation corpus validation, remaining planned-folder inspection, key-file sampling, entity-quality review, and final graph-metadata health scoring after phases 001-005 landed.

focus: Post-implementation active-corpus rescan and raw metric refresh
noveltyJustification: This pass established the new 364-packet active baseline after implementation and showed that graph integrity stayed clean while status and freshness metrics improved sharply.
ruledOut: Reusing the older 344-file totals without a fresh active-corpus count

focus: Before-versus-after comparison for the original eight research questions
noveltyJustification: Wave 4 converted the raw rescan into direct deltas against Wave 1 and showed that key-file resolution, status fidelity, trigger hygiene, and freshness all improved while entity-cap saturation became the new bottleneck.
ruledOut: Treating entity de-duplication as the end of entity-quality work

focus: Inventory the remaining planned folders and prepare the manual sample
noveltyJustification: This pass split the 56 planned folders into incomplete, parent-only, and checklist-complete buckets so the manual sample could target the right failure modes instead of treating all planned packets as equivalent.
ruledOut: Assuming every remaining planned packet was either stale or parser-broken

focus: Manual review of a 10-folder planned sample plus full-set stale-pocket check
noveltyJustification: The manual sample proved the remaining planned set is mostly real, while the full-set follow-up isolated three clearly stale doc-alignment packets and two contract-bound packets that only lack implementation-summary.md.
ruledOut: Calling the two complete-checklist skilled-agent packets parser regressions before checking their missing implementation summaries

focus: Manual 50-entry key-file sample across 10 random folders
noveltyJustification: The sample established that key-file quality is much cleaner than Wave 1 but not perfect: 47 of 50 sampled entries resolved, and the misses were concentrated in stale memory-era or cross-track references rather than the earlier junk-token flood.
ruledOut: Assuming the improved global resolve rate meant the random sample would be 50 of 50 clean

focus: Residual global key-file miss families after sanitization
noveltyJustification: This pass showed that the remaining miss classes are now dominated by path-like references, phrase-like carryovers, and shell-command snippets that the current predicate still classifies too loosely.
ruledOut: Treating all remaining path-like misses as equivalent when obsolete memory paths and command snippets are now different cleanup targets

focus: Global entity-quality metrics after de-duplication
noveltyJustification: Wave 4 proved the duplicate-name fix held across the live corpus, but it also exposed near-universal 16-entity saturation as the next precision problem.
ruledOut: Equating zero duplicate rows with healthy entity prioritization

focus: Manual entity sample plus residual anomaly inspection
noveltyJustification: The random sample came back clean, which shifted the residual question from broad entity noise to a tiny set of specific extraction artifacts: one code-fence language token and two slash-joined headings.
ruledOut: Opening another broad entity-cleanup wave before isolating the exact surviving anomaly patterns

focus: Heuristic health scoring and stale-metadata anomaly review
noveltyJustification: This pass collapsed the post-implementation evidence into one scorecard and isolated the two remaining maintenance anomalies that still need attention: three stale doc-alignment packets and one hyphenated status value.
ruledOut: Treating the remaining stale packets as parser regressions before checking their source-doc lag

focus: Final post-implementation synthesis and next-phase targeting
noveltyJustification: No new defect class appeared here; the value came from turning the validated corpus metrics into one clear maintenance recommendation plus a sharply scoped follow-on phase target.
ruledOut: Starting another discovery-only loop instead of converting the residual evidence into explicit next-phase goals

note: Wave 4 rescanned the post-implementation corpus, compared the original eight questions before versus after remediation, and narrowed the remaining work to stale-backfill cleanup plus one residual-hygiene follow-on phase.

reason: User requested iterations 36-55 focused on post-fix corpus-health revalidation after F11-F15, with early stop if three consecutive iterations stayed below the 0.1 novelty threshold.

focus: Post-fix key_files noise revalidation
noveltyJustification: The rerun confirmed the intended fix held, but only refined the existing residual key-file families rather than discovering a new defect class.
ruledOut: Reopening command-snippet hygiene as the next parser target

focus: Post-fix status, trigger, and freshness revalidation
noveltyJustification: This pass confirmed the normalization and trigger-cap fixes held and only narrowed the remaining status caveats to explicit frontmatter intent and contract-bound packets.
ruledOut: Treating the remaining planned values as a normalization regression

focus: Post-fix entity precision revalidation and convergence
noveltyJustification: The final pass improved the residual entity counts and health score, but it still only refined already-known hygiene work rather than opening another broad discovery area.
ruledOut: Opening another broad discovery wave before residual hygiene implementation

note: Wave 5 revalidated the post-fix corpus after F11-F15, raised the heuristic health score to 91/100, and stopped early once three consecutive low-novelty iterations confirmed that the remaining issues were implementation-only hygiene.
